issues.description: unmap_channel_page (address_space.rs:411) does not bounds-check that 'va' falls within [CHANNEL_SHM_BASE, CHANNEL_SHM_END). Current callers always pass values just returned by map_channel_page so this is safe in practice, but a debug_assert would prevent misuse if the function is called from new sites in the future.

unmap_page_inner's doc comment says 'caller is responsible for a bulk invalidate after unmapping all pages', but unmap_channel_page callers in sys_handle_send and sys_channel_create do not issue a TLB invalidation. This is correct because the target process has not started (target.started == false check), so no TLB entries exist for its ASID. A brief comment at the unmap_channel_page call sites noting why TLB invalidation is unnecessary would prevent future confusion.

In the test model (handle_send_rollback.rs:193), the variable '_va_b' uses underscore prefix (suppress-unused convention) but is actually used in the unmap call. Rename to 'va_b' for clarity, matching the kernel code.

sharedStateObservations.description: The kernel-auditor skill (Step 5) says 'Write a test that demonstrates the bug (the test must FAIL before the fix)'. For bugs in code that can't be imported on the host, the worker used model-based testing with separate buggy/fixed model functions — both pass because they test the model, not the kernel directly. This pattern is now used in 3 out of 3 M1 bug fixes. The skill should acknowledge model-based TDD as an acceptable approach for kernel logic not importable on the host, per the worker's skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handle_send_rollback.rs defines handle_send_phase2_buggy and handle_send_phase2_fixed — same pattern as channel_create_leak.rs and scheduling_context_create_leak.rs. Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly flags this.

sharedStateObservations.description: The discovered issue about sys_channel_create's PTE leak (same rollback pattern needed) was proactively fixed in a follow-up commit (9d83ab7). This cross-feature discovery pattern — where fixing one bug reveals an analogous bug in a sibling syscall — should be noted as a risk pattern for future audit work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[0] describes the sys_channel_create PTE leak. Commit 9d83ab7 (one commit after d9a32a2) fixes it with the same unmap_channel_page rollback pattern.

summary: The fix correctly addresses both failure paths in sys_handle_send Phase 2: (1) second map_channel_page failure now unmaps the first page, (2) handles.insert failure now unmaps both pages. The new unmap_channel_page method properly clears PTEs without freeing physical frames (channel retains ownership). 7 well-structured model-based tests cover both failure scenarios, success path, non-channel path, and started-target rejection. No blocking issues found.
